channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The ballot’s mailed but you die before Election Day. Now what?
item.description: Some states allow them to be counted, and some don't. But most states have never addressed the question.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: US long-range B-2 stealth bombers target underground bunkers of Yemen's Houthi rebels
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. long-range B-2 stealth bombers launched airstrikes early Thursday morning targeting underground bunkers used by Yemen's Houthi rebels, officials said. It wasn't immediately clear what damage was done in the strikes.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Dollar General employee finds puppies dumped in trash amid cold Arkansas weather
item.description: Three puppies, less than a week old, were abandoned this week inside of a Dollar General Market dumpster, according to Arkansas authorities.
item.dc:creator: Elena Ramirez, ABC 4

item.title: Archdiocese of Los Angeles agrees to pay $880 million to victims of clergy sexual abuse
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Archdiocese of Los Angeles has agreed to pay $880 million to victims of clergy sexual abuse dating back decades, in what an attorney said was the largest single child sex abuse settlement with a Catholic archdiocese, it was announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US warns of growing North Korean military support for Russia's war
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The U.S. and its allies are “alarmed” by North Korea’s nuclear and missile threats as well as its increasing military support for Russia’s war in Ukraine, a senior U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Family of Erik and Lyle Menendez call for their release and say they're victims who were vilified
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Multiple generations of family members of Erik and Lyle Menendez pleaded for the brothers' release from prison on Wednesday, saying they deserve to be free despite life sentences for the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills because they had been “brutalized” and sexuall
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Navy jet located after crashing near Mount Rainier in Washington
item.description: The U.S. Navy located an EA-18G Growler jet on Wednesday that crashed near Mount Rainier in Washington on Tuesday, though the status of the two-person crew is unknown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris' interview with Fox News is marked by testy exchanges over immigration and more
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris engaged in a combative interview with Fox News on Wednesday, sparring with anchor Bret Baier on immigration and shifting policy positions while asserting that if elected, she would not represent a continuation of Joe Biden's presidency.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 3 dead, at least 4 injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and at least four were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman accuses Diddy of retaliatory sexual assault in new lawsuit
item.description: A California woman has filed a new lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs, alleging that he and his entourage sexually assaulted her in 2018 as an act of retaliation.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about shaken baby syndrome as a Texas man could be first in US executed over it
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A Texas man this week could become the first person executed in the U.S. for a murder conviction tied to the diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wife, mother, attorney shares hardships to inspire others
item.description: Her father grew up in Mexico City, was well-educated and moved to the United States to further his education and career as a chemical and petroleum engineer.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Jovic, NewsNation

item.title: Lyft launches voter transportation initiative
item.description: “Lyft believes transportation access should never be a barrier for any citizen seeking to vote," Lyft Chief Policy Officer Jerry Golden said in the release.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Israel professor at Columbia barred from campus after harassing and intimidating other employees
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An outspoken pro-Israel professor at Columbia University has been temporarily barred from campus, a university spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal money to help states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton nears $2 billion
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government has approved nearly $2 billion in assistance across six states to help people hit hard by hurricanes Helene and Milton, the White House said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Honey prices increase as Rio Grande Valley bees struggle
item.description: "It's very challenging with all the new problems we’ve had from imported pests and diseases," says Brownsville beekeeper Glenn Simpson of Bay Bees RGV.
item.dc:creator: Blake Reagan Brown, NewsNation

item.title: Montana man camping near Big Sky found dead in tent after ‘vicious attack,’ police say
item.description: A man, later identified as Dustin Kjersem, 35, was found dead in a tent last weekend near Big Sky, Montana, and investigators are calling the case a homicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clint Eastwood's daughter arrested in Beverly Hills in domestic violence case
item.description: Actor Francesca Eastwood, daughter of legendary actor Clint Eastwood, was arrested in Beverly Hills over the weekend on suspicion of felony domestic violence, according to reporting from the Los Angeles Times.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, ABC 4

item.title: Do you keep money in payment apps? Here's why you probably shouldn't
item.description: As the use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Michelle Obama will headline an Atlanta rally aimed at boosting voter turnout
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former first lady Michelle Obama will headline a rally in Atlanta a week before the Nov. 5 election alongside celebrities and civic leaders focusing on engaging younger and first-time voters, as well as voters of color. The Oct.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, KTLA 5

item.title: Long lost sisters meet for the first time in person
item.description: Two long-lost sisters who met for the first time in person say their love will continue even after this life.
item.dc:creator: Alina Lee, Fox 5

item.title: The Super Bowl will return to Atlanta in 2028: Where will it be played before that?
item.description: Atlanta has secured the 2028 Super Bowl - here are the other cities that will host the NFL's championship game before then.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Some Social Security recipients will receive an extra check next month
item.description: It may be hard to believe, but November is fast approaching. With it will come Election Day, Thanksgiving, and, for some seven million Supplemental Security Income beneficiaries, an extra check. It's all thanks to a quirk in the 2024 calendar.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new lawsuits, including man who was 16 at the time
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean “Diddy” Combs was hit Monday with a new wave of lawsuits accusing him of raping women, sexually assaulting men and molesting a 16-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Aidan Hutchinson likely down 4-6 months with broken leg, Lions coach Dan Campbell says
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Aidan Hutchinson is likely done playing this year because of the leg injury he sustained against the Cowboys, but surgery to repair his fractured tibia and fibula “went great” and Lions coach Dan Campbell said Monday he isn't ready to declare his star pass rusher out for the
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Glen Powell films 'Chad Powers' during halftime at UGA game as crowd boos
item.description: Glen Powell took the field during halftime at the University of Georgia’s game against Mississippi State on Saturday, while filming his upcoming comedy series "Chad Powers.
item.dc:creator: TMX, KTLA 5

item.title: NJ Transit operator killed, passengers injured after train hits tree
item.description: An NJ Transit train operator is dead and nearly two dozen passengers are injured after a train struck a tree on the tracks Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, WGN 9

item.title: Group tied to polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service land
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862. According to the Montezuma County Sheriff's Office, the actions have resulted in a property dispute between the Free Land Holders and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KTLA 5

item.title: Why are scientists so interested in Europa?
item.description: NASA's Europa Clipper soared into space Monday, marking a major step toward exploring one of the most intriguing moons in our solar system.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: List: Here are the nearly 500 Big Lots stores that have or will be closed this year
item.description: Since filing for bankruptcy protection earlier this year, Big Lots has slated nearly 500 stores for closure - some of which have already closed their doors for good.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: From the pulpit, Harris calls out Trump for hurricane misinformation. Biden surveys Florida's damage
item.description: GREENVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Kamala Harris used an appearance Sunday before a largely Black church audience in battleground North Carolina to call out Donald Trump for spreading misinformation about the government's hurricane response.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rising chocolate prices impact Halloween candy choices
item.description: Despite rising chocolate and candy prices, Americans are expected to spend $3.5 million on Halloween sweets this year.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, ABC 4
